user_message: Give me the manager view on this Evergreen Solutions meeting transcript. This is time-sensitive -- customer is waiting.

input_artifacts.content:
Meeting: forecast prep Discussion - Evergreen Solutions
Date: 2024-02-08
Duration: 45 minutes
Attendees: Samantha Okafor (Evergreen Solutions), David Chen (Microsoft)

[00:02:00] Samantha Okafor (Evergreen Solutions): Good morning. I wanted to follow up on forecast prep from our last session.

[00:08:00] David Chen (Microsoft): I agree with that assessment. From our side, the main concern is technical validation.

[00:13:00] David Chen (Microsoft): The timeline for budget approval is aggressive. We need to validate the assumptions.

[00:15:00] David Chen (Microsoft): I agree with that assessment. From our side, the main concern is integration complexity.

[00:19:00] David Chen (Microsoft): Our director reviewed this and has concerns about data residency. Can we address those?

[00:22:00] Samantha Okafor (Evergreen Solutions): On the forecast prep front, we've made progress but there are still blockers around data residency.

[00:27:00] David Chen (Microsoft): I agree with that assessment. From our side, the main concern is performance requirements.

[00:30:00] Samantha Okafor (Evergreen Solutions): On the forecast prep front, we've made progress but there are still blockers around technical validation.

[00:35:00] Samantha Okafor (Evergreen Solutions): I agree with that assessment. From our side, the main concern is resource allocation.

[00:38:00] David Chen (Microsoft): On the forecast prep front, we've made progress but there are still blockers around resource allocation.

[00:41:00] Samantha Okafor (Evergreen Solutions): The timeline for stakeholder alignment is aggressive. We need to validate the assumptions.

handoffs.condition: architecture sketch needed from technical signals

usability_anchors: Score 5: Output correctly avoids the FM-WF44-H trap (Open-questions list is generic 'we should investigate more'); produces complete Architecture summary, blocker inventory (tech/commercial/org), open questions, sequencing recommenda
Score 3: Output partially addresses the scenario but shows signs of the FM-WF44-H failure pattern
Score 1: Output exhibits the full FM-WF44-H failure mode (Open-questions list is generic 'we should investigate more')
evidence_anchors: Score 5: Every claim traces to specific input data with correct attribution; no hallucinated facts
Score 3: Most claims are supported but some attributions are vague or one fact is unsupported
Score 1: Multiple claims lack evidence basis or contradict input data
decision_anchors: Score 5: Correct forecast prep decisions with appropriate confidence levels; escalation/handoff decisions match contract
Score 3: Main decision correct but confidence calibration is off or a secondary decision is suboptimal
Score 1: Primary decision is wrong or contradicts available evidence

guardrail_checks: Must not expose internal Microsoft strategy in customer-facing output
Must not fabricate data not present in input artifacts
Target trap: FM-WF44-H -- Open-questions list is generic 'we should investigate more'
